callRationale: Started with a broad official-docs-and-benchmark search across Firecrawl, Exa, Parallel, and Tavily domains to discover canonical comparison evidence.
Added a targeted Tavily docs search because the broad call undercovered docs.tavily.com and Tavily needed first-party evidence.
Added a targeted Exa docs search to capture Exa's own response-shaping, MCP, highlights, freshness, and search-mode controls.
Added a targeted Parallel docs and benchmark search to capture its dense-excerpt, single-call, WISER benchmark, and API-shape claims.
Added a targeted Firecrawl docs search to capture search plus scrapeOptions, MCP, scraping, and structured extraction details.
Fetched the canonical pages in one batch to ground the final comparison in full page evidence rather than only search-result summaries.
strengths: Retrieved all expected domains: docs.firecrawl.dev, exa.ai, parallel.ai, and docs.tavily.com.
Evidence covered both docs and benchmark/comparison pages, including provider-specific MCP/search behavior.
Exa advanced search controls allowed source restriction, fresh retrieval, summaries, highlights, and content budgets.
The final batched fetch provided canonical page evidence for the answer.
weaknesses: The initial broad search heavily favored Parallel, Exa, and Firecrawl and missed Tavily first-party docs, requiring a targeted follow-up.
Some highlight snippets were noisy or truncated, so the final answer relied more on summaries and fetched canonical pages.
Benchmark evidence for Parallel and Exa-vs-Tavily is vendor-published, so exact leaderboard claims should be treated as directional.
notableFailures: One pre-provider harness invocation failed because the args file path was relative to the wrong workspace; subsequent calls used absolute evals/work paths.
No neutral third-party benchmark page covering all four providers was retrieved; official/vendor benchmark pages were used with caveats.
